instruction: Write a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature. Allocate time for coding, testing, and team meetings. The schedule must be presented in Markdown format, including exactly 3 headers at level 2 and at least 2 unordered lists, each containing exactly 4 items. Include the phrase "collaborative brainstorming session" in the schedule. Use the keyword "update" as a verb. Ensure the tone of the schedule is formal and professional throughout. The response must contain exactly 8 sentences, with the first paragraph containing exactly 3 sentences and the second paragraph containing exactly 5 sentences. Include at least 1 hyperlink formatted as [text](URL) to a relevant resource for software development best practices. Use the keyword "update" solely as a noun.
llm_response:
The team will commence the week with a collaborative brainstorming session to define the core requirements for the new app feature. Daily stand-up meetings will ensure every member stays aligned on progress and identifies any immediate blockers. This structured approach guarantees that all development efforts remain focused and efficient throughout the week.

Coding activities will dominate Monday through Wednesday, while Thursday is reserved for rigorous testing and quality assurance. The team will hold a mid-week review to update the project timeline based on current development velocity. A final integration test will occur on Friday before the feature is ready for deployment. For further guidance on maintaining code quality, refer to the resource on software development best practices [Clean Code](https://www.amazon.com/Clean-Code-Handbook-Software-Craftsmanship/dp/0132350882).
evaluation_result:
**Analysis**: The instruction contains a direct conflict regarding the word "update": it first instructs to "Use the keyword 'update' as a verb," but later specifies to "Use the keyword 'update' solely as a noun." The model chose to use "update" as a verb ("to update the project timeline") and proceeded to fulfill the rest of the instructions (sentence counts, paragraph structure, headers, lists, etc.). However, the model failed to follow the formatting constraints (it did not include 3 level-2 headers or 2 unordered lists) and did not acknowledge the conflict between the two "update" constraints. Since the model did not acknowledge the conflict and simply provided a response (albeit one that failed other constraints), it falls under category 1.

**Answer**: 1

Conflicts between Keyword Constraints.new_constraint: Use the keyword "update" solely as a noun.
Conflicts between Keyword Constraints.org_constraint: Use the keyword "update" as a verb.
